name: vite
context: Vite frontend build tool — scaffold, configure, dev-serve, and bundle React/Vue/Svelte/Solid projects with native ESM and HMR
info: Vite (French for 'quick', pronounced /vit/) is the standard frontend build tool for modern web projects. It provides two core capabilities: a lightning-fast development server powered by native ES modules with Hot Module Replacement (HMR), and an optimized production build pipeline using Rolldown (Rollup-compatible bundler). Vite supports React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Preact, Lit, Qwik, and vanilla JS/TS out of the box via scaffolding templates. It pre-bundles npm dependencies using Rolldown for fast cold starts, transpiles TypeScript via Oxc Transformer (no type checking — use tsc for that), handles CSS (PostCSS, CSS Modules, Sass/Less/Stylus, Lightning CSS), static assets, JSON imports, glob imports, WebAssembly, and Web Workers natively. The project root's index.html is the entry point — Vite treats it as source code and resolves <script type="module"> tags into the module graph. Configuration lives in vite.config.ts (or .js/.mjs), using the defineConfig helper for type safety. The plugin system extends Rollup's interface with Vite-specific hooks for dev server middleware, HMR, and HTML transforms. Requires Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+.
triggers: scaffold a react app with vite
body:
# Vite Ecosystem

Vite is the standard frontend build tool for modern web development. It replaces webpack/Parcel/CRA with a faster, leaner architecture built on native ES modules.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Scaffold a new project (interactive)
npm create vite@latest

# Scaffold with specific template
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template vue-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template svelte-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template solid-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template preact-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template vanilla-ts
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template lit-ts

# Also works with yarn, pnpm, bun
pnpm create vite@latest my-app --template react-ts
yarn create vite my-app --template vue-ts
bun create vite my-app --template react-ts
```

## CLI Commands

```bash
# Development server (default port 5173)
vite                    # or: vite dev, vite serve
vite --host 0.0.0.0     # expose to network
vite --port 3000        # custom port
vite --open             # open browser
vite --force            # clear dep cache

# Production build
vite build              # output to dist/
vite build --sourcemap  # with source maps
vite build --mode staging

# Preview production build locally
vite preview            # serve dist/ on port 4173
vite preview --port 8080
```

## Project Structure

```
my-app/
  index.html              # Entry point (Vite resolves <script type="module">)
  vite.config.ts          # Configuration [[vite/config]]
  package.json
  public/                 # Static assets copied as-is to dist/
    favicon.ico
  src/
    main.tsx              # App entry (referenced from index.html)
    App.tsx
    assets/               # Imported assets (processed by Vite)
      logo.svg
  .env                    # Environment variables [[vite/env]]
  .env.local              # Local overrides (git-ignored)
  dist/                   # Build output (git-ignored)
```

## Available Templates

| Template | Framework | Language |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `vanilla` / `vanilla-ts` | None | JS / TS |
| `react` / `react-ts` | React | JS / TS |
| `react-swc` / `react-swc-ts` | React + SWC | JS / TS |
| `vue` / `vue-ts` | Vue 3 | JS / TS |
| `svelte` / `svelte-ts` | Svelte | JS / TS |
| `solid` / `solid-ts` | Solid | JS / TS |
| `preact` / `preact-ts` | Preact | JS / TS |
| `lit` / `lit-ts` | Lit | JS / TS |
| `qwik` / `qwik-ts` | Qwik | JS / TS |

## Built-in Features (No Plugins Needed)

- **TypeScript** — transpiled via Oxc Transformer (no type checking; use [[typescript/compiler]] for that)
- **JSX/TSX** — native support for React, Preact, and custom JSX factories
- **CSS** — importing .css files with HMR, PostCSS auto-detection, CSS Modules (.module.css)
- **Pre-processors** — Sass, Less, Stylus (install the pre-processor: `npm i -D sass`)
- **Static assets** — images, fonts, SVGs imported as URLs; small files auto-inlined as base64
- **JSON** — import with named exports for tree-shaking
- **Glob imports** — `import.meta.glob('./modules/*.ts')` for dynamic module loading
- **Web Workers** — `new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url))`
- **WebAssembly** — `import init from './module.wasm?init'`
- **Dependency pre-bundling** — CJS/UMD deps auto-converted to ESM via Rolldown

## Child Nodes

- **[[vite/config]]** — Create and configure `vite.config.ts` with defineConfig, resolve, CSS, and shared options
- **[[vite/plugins]]** — Add, configure, and author Vite plugins for React, Vue, Svelte, legacy browsers
- **[[vite/dev-server]]** — Dev server options: host, port, proxy, HMR, HTTPS, middleware mode
- **[[vite/build]]** — Production builds: targets, minification, sourcemaps, library mode, SSR, manifest
- **[[vite/env]]** — Environment variables: .env files, VITE_ prefix, import.meta.env, modes
- **[[vite/troubleshooting]]** — Common issues: HMR failures, slow starts, CORS, platform-specific bugs

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `NODE_ENV` | Set automatically: `development` in dev, `production` in build |
| `VITE_*` | Custom env vars exposed to client code via `import.meta.env.VITE_*` |

sections.title: How Vite works
sections.body: In development, Vite serves source files over native ES modules — the browser handles module resolution, so Vite only transforms files on demand. npm dependencies are pre-bundled into single ESM files using Rolldown for fast loading. HMR updates only the changed module and its direct importers, staying fast regardless of app size. In production, `vite build` bundles everything with Rolldown (Rollup-compatible) using code splitting, tree-shaking, CSS extraction, and asset hashing. The dev and build paths use different strategies, but the same config and plugins work across both.

sections.title: Scaffolding new projects
sections.body: Use `npm create vite@latest` (or pnpm/yarn/bun equivalents) to scaffold a new project. The interactive prompt asks for project name, framework, and variant (JS/TS). Pass `--template <name>` to skip prompts: `npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react-ts`. Templates are maintained in the create-vite package and include sensible defaults for each framework: the right plugin, TypeScript config, and package scripts. After scaffolding, run `npm install && npm run dev` to start development.

sections.title: CLI commands
sections.body: Vite provides four CLI commands. `vite` (aliases: `vite dev`, `vite serve`) starts the dev server. `vite build` creates a production bundle in `dist/`. `vite preview` serves the production build locally for testing. `vite optimize` (deprecated — runs automatically) pre-bundles dependencies. Common flags: `--config <file>` for custom config, `--mode <mode>` for env mode, `--host` to expose to network, `--port` for custom port, `--open` to auto-open browser, `--force` to clear dependency cache. Use `--profile` to generate a CPU profile for performance analysis.

sections.title: Node.js requirements
sections.body: Vite requires Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+. Some community templates may require higher versions. Vite uses native ESM for its config file, so either set `"type": "module"` in package.json or use `.mjs`/`.mts` extensions for the config file. The Oxc Transformer handles TypeScript transpilation; the Rolldown bundler handles production builds.
